sources_exist.note: Identity and all four deterministic dataset_inputs match the assigned manifest and derived dataset exactly. The CT Acquisitions, Lawn & Landscape GPS, and Yellowstone URLs resolve, but inputs.current_adoption_pct.url is inaccessible and inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url resolves only to an unrelated/empty Main Street Wealth landing experience rather than the cited landscaping analysis. The assigned ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is not an unresolved issue: the nonzero stored-versus-recomputed differences on B, M, S, and V are explicitly within the orchestrator tolerance.

figures_match_sources.note: The exact cited pages fail to support five score-bearing claims: the GPS article contains neither 42% penetration nor the claimed 2023/accounting-software trajectory; the Main Street Wealth page does not expose the owner-age or succession assertions; CT Acquisitions does not substantiate the stated 14-platform count and explicitly calls Ruppert non-PE; and the selected buy/exit values are discretionary adjustments from ranges rather than sourced medians. Yellowstone supports monthly fixed-scope billing, a 12-month auto-renewal, and 3-5% annual increases, but not the asserted 60/25/15 industry revenue mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: All role fractions, pi_dist=0.35, and pi_moat=0.40 are bounded with concrete and industry-plausible rationales. However, t50_years=8 is score-affecting and its declared derivation materially relies on the unsupported historical_analogs claim that GPS reached 42% by 2023 after about 13 years and accounting software reached 77% after about 15 years; the cited 2017 article supplies neither benchmark. A rerun needs a complete auditable diffusion chain before this judgment can pass.

rubric_consistent.note: The physical-field-labor treatment, durable terminal class, no-proxy separation, and t50 uncertainty disclosure follow the frozen rubric. But B uses active_consolidators=14 despite the cited page not documenting that count and despite the run's list including entities not established as PE-backed active add-on platforms; M uses 5.5x and 8.5x as selected conservative points rather than evidence-backed medians actually paid for the rubric's target bands. These are score-affecting rubric-input failures.

cross_checks_honest.note: Uplift consistency and the DURABLE terminal-value discussion are candid, and PE activity is not used in the role-based V rationale. The no-proxy cross-check nevertheless says A was derived from landscaping GPS and management-software diffusion benchmarks that the cited source does not establish. The pricing-deflation discussion also presents a 60% commercial revenue share as established although the audited Yellowstone citation supplies contract mechanics, not an industry billing-mix percentage.

source_audits.note: The landscaping multiples report resolves and names some active platforms, but it does not document the run's 14-platform count or full last-24-month add-on list. It explicitly describes Ruppert as family-owned and not PE-backed as of 2026-Q1, contrary to including Ruppert in a count defined as PE-backed platforms.

The page supports a 5.0x-7.0x adjusted-EBITDA range for $2M-$10M revenue financial-buyer transactions and reports other observed medians, but it does not establish 5.5x as the median actually paid for the rubric's $1M-$10M EBITDA target band. The run chooses 5.5x as a discretionary conservative adjustment from the range.

The exact URL did not return an accessible cited article, so its quoted 32%/11%/30% use-frequency figures cannot be audited. Separate Aspire/NALP pages can support the 83%-not-using/17%-using AI result, but they are not the cited URL and do not cure this failed citation occurrence.

The page supports a 7.5x-10.0x adjusted-EBITDA platform range and reports 8.7x average for disclosed 2025 platform transactions plus an approximately 8.1x BrightView comp. It does not report 8.5x as a median; the run selects that score-bearing value as a discretionary conservative adjustment.

The cited page is a sponsored 2017 Fleet Management Guide article. It says landscaping adoption mirrored fleet-industry adoption and predicted majority use within a few years, but gives no 42% penetration figure, no 2023 measurement, no approximately 13-year diffusion interval, and no 77% accounting-software figure or 15-year trajectory.

The requested resource does not resolve to accessible landscaping-analysis content; only a generic Main Street Wealth home-services landing page is exposed. It therefore cannot support the estimated 30% age-60-plus share or the quoted assertion that most $5M-$25M EBITDA founder-led landscaping operators lack a successor.

Yellowstone supports defined services billed monthly, property-manager purchasing authority, standard 12-month agreements with automatic renewal, and automatic annual price increases of 3%-5%. It does not establish the run's industry-wide 60% commercial, 25% residential, and 15% design-build/enhancement revenue shares or multi-year prevalence, so the full cited pricing-mix claim is unsupported.

reasons: At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.lawnandlandscape.com/article/adoption-of-gps-tracking-technology-increasing-as-landscaping-businesses-understand-the-value/), the 2017 article does not report 42% GPS penetration in 2023, a 13-year diffusion interval, or 77% accounting-software adoption after 15 years. Re-run with primary or accessible survey sources that establish each analog's adoption percentage, population, date, and elapsed-time basis, then rebuild the t50_years research chain.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.lawnandlandscape.com/article/2026-tech-report/), the exact cited page is inaccessible and cannot support the quoted 2026 use-frequency figures. Re-run with an accessible primary survey URL that directly supports the selected definition and value of material AI adoption; do not combine a light-use survey with the Aspire adoption figure without separately auditable citations.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://mainstreetwealth.ai/resources/landscaping-m-n-a-2026), the cited landscaping resource does not resolve to accessible supporting content. Re-run with accessible industry-specific owner-age evidence and a separately auditable source for the machine-readable succession-shortage assertion, including population and age cutoff.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/landscaping-ma-multiples-2026/), the page does not substantiate the stated count of 14 PE-backed platforms active in add-ons during the last 24 months, and it explicitly describes included Ruppert as not PE-backed. Re-run with one auditable transaction/sponsor citation per counted platform and enforce the rubric's PE-backed and recency criteria.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/landscaping-ma-multiples-2026/), 5.5x and 8.5x are discretionary points selected from broad ranges rather than sourced medians actually paid for the rubric's target bands. Re-run using transaction evidence matched to EBITDA size, normalized owner compensation, date, and statistic, or label and justify any fallback research chain without presenting an adjusted range point as a sourced median.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.yellowstonelandscape.com/blog/everything-youve-wanted-know-about-commercial-landscape-maintenance-contracts), the page supports contract mechanics but not the score-bearing 60/25/15 revenue mix repeated in pi_dist and pricing_model_deflation. Re-run with an accessible industry benchmark for the billing/revenue mix and keep the Yellowstone source only for the contract terms it actually establishes.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V
